section.title: DG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysics Class Reference

listitem.para: static TweenerCore< Vector3, Vector3, VectorOptions > DOMove (this Rigidbody target, Vector3 endValue, float duration, bool snapping=false)
Tweens a Rigidbody's position to the given value.
static TweenerCore< Vector3, Vector3, VectorOptions > DOMoveX (this Rigidbody target, float endValue, float duration, bool snapping=false)
Tweens a Rigidbody's X position to the given value.
static TweenerCore< Vector3, Vector3, VectorOptions > DOMoveY (this Rigidbody target, float endValue, float duration, bool snapping=false)
Tweens a Rigidbody's Y position to the given value.
static TweenerCore< Vector3, Vector3, VectorOptions > DOMoveZ (this Rigidbody target, float endValue, float duration, bool snapping=false)
Tweens a Rigidbody's Z position to the given value.
static TweenerCore< Quaternion, Vector3, QuaternionOptions > DORotate (this Rigidbody target, Vector3 endValue, float duration, RotateMode mode=RotateMode.Fast)
Tweens a Rigidbody's rotation to the given value.
static TweenerCore< Quaternion, Vector3, QuaternionOptions > DOLookAt (this Rigidbody target, Vector3 towards, float duration, AxisConstraint axisConstraint=AxisConstraint.None, Vector3? up=null)
Tweens a Rigidbody's rotation so that it will look towards the given position.
static Sequence DOJump (this Rigidbody target, Vector3 endValue, float jumpPower, int numJumps, float duration, bool snapping=false)
Tweens a Rigidbody's position to the given value, while also applying a jump effect along the Y axis.

static TweenerCore< Vector3, Path, PathOptions > DOPath (this Rigidbody target, Vector3[] path, float duration, PathType pathType=PathType.Linear, PathMode pathMode=PathMode.Full3D, int resolution=10, Color? gizmoColor=null)
Tweens a Rigidbody's position through the given path waypoints, using the chosen path algorithm.

static TweenerCore< Vector3, Path, PathOptions > DOLocalPath (this Rigidbody target, Vector3[] path, float duration, PathType pathType=PathType.Linear, PathMode pathMode=PathMode.Full3D, int resolution=10, Color? gizmoColor=null)
Tweens a Rigidbody's localPosition through the given path waypoints, using the chosen path algorithm.

section.section:
Member Function Documentation
DOJump() DOJumpDG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysics DG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysicsDOJump
Sequence DG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysics.DOJump (this Rigidbody target, Vector3 endValue, float jumpPower, int numJumps, float duration, bool snapping = false )[static]
Tweens a Rigidbody's position to the given value, while also applying a jump effect along the Y axis.
Returns a Sequence instead of a Tweener. Also stores the Rigidbody as the tween's target so it can be used for filtered operations
Parameters
endValue
The end value to reach
jumpPower
Power of the jump (the max height of the jump is represented by this plus the final Y offset)
numJumps
Total number of jumps
duration
The duration of the tween
snapping
If TRUE the tween will smoothly snap all values to integers
DOLocalPath() DOLocalPathDG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysics DG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysicsDOLocalPath
TweenerCore< Vector3, Path, PathOptions > DG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysics.DOLocalPath (this Rigidbody target, Vector3[] path, float duration, PathType pathType = PathType::Linear , PathMode pathMode = PathMode::Full3D , int resolution = 10 , Color? gizmoColor = null )[static]
Tweens a Rigidbody's localPosition through the given path waypoints, using the chosen path algorithm.
Also stores the Rigidbody as the tween's target so it can be used for filtered operations
NOTE: to tween a rigidbody correctly it should be set to kinematic at least while being tweened.
BEWARE: doesn't work on Windows Phone store (waiting for Unity to fix their own bug). If you plan to publish there you should use a regular transform.DOLocalPath.
Parameters
path
The waypoint to go through
duration
The duration of the tween
pathType
The type of path: Linear (straight path), CatmullRom (curved CatmullRom path) or CubicBezier (curved with control points)
pathMode
The path mode: 3D, side-scroller 2D, top-down 2D
resolution
The resolution of the path: higher resolutions make for more detailed curved paths but are more expensive. Defaults to 10, but a value of 5 is usually enough if you don't have dramatic long curves between waypoints
gizmoColor
The color of the path (shown when gizmos are active in the Play panel and the tween is running)
DOLookAt() DOLookAtDG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysics DG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysicsDOLookAt
TweenerCore< Quaternion, Vector3, QuaternionOptions > DG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysics.DOLookAt (this Rigidbody target, Vector3 towards, float duration, AxisConstraint axisConstraint = AxisConstraint::None , Vector3? up = null )[static]
Tweens a Rigidbody's rotation so that it will look towards the given position.
Also stores the rigidbody as the tween's target so it can be used for filtered operations
Parameters
towards
The position to look at
duration
The duration of the tween
axisConstraint
Eventual axis constraint for the rotation
up
The vector that defines in which direction up is (default: Vector3.up)
DOMove() DOMoveDG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysics DG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysicsDOMove
TweenerCore< Vector3, Vector3, VectorOptions > DG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysics.DOMove (this Rigidbody target, Vector3 endValue, float duration, bool snapping = false )[static]
Tweens a Rigidbody's position to the given value.
Also stores the rigidbody as the tween's target so it can be used for filtered operations
Parameters
endValue
The end value to reach
duration
The duration of the tween
snapping
If TRUE the tween will smoothly snap all values to integers
DOMoveX() DOMoveXDG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysics DG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysicsDOMoveX
TweenerCore< Vector3, Vector3, VectorOptions > DG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysics.DOMoveX (this Rigidbody target, float endValue, float duration, bool snapping = false )[static]
Tweens a Rigidbody's X position to the given value.
Also stores the rigidbody as the tween's target so it can be used for filtered operations
Parameters
endValue
The end value to reach
duration
The duration of the tween
snapping
If TRUE the tween will smoothly snap all values to integers
DOMoveY() DOMoveYDG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysics DG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysicsDOMoveY
TweenerCore< Vector3, Vector3, VectorOptions > DG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysics.DOMoveY (this Rigidbody target, float endValue, float duration, bool snapping = false )[static]
Tweens a Rigidbody's Y position to the given value.
Also stores the rigidbody as the tween's target so it can be used for filtered operations
Parameters
endValue
The end value to reach
duration
The duration of the tween
snapping
If TRUE the tween will smoothly snap all values to integers
DOMoveZ() DOMoveZDG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysics DG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysicsDOMoveZ
TweenerCore< Vector3, Vector3, VectorOptions > DG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysics.DOMoveZ (this Rigidbody target, float endValue, float duration, bool snapping = false )[static]
Tweens a Rigidbody's Z position to the given value.
Also stores the rigidbody as the tween's target so it can be used for filtered operations
Parameters
endValue
The end value to reach
duration
The duration of the tween
snapping
If TRUE the tween will smoothly snap all values to integers
DOPath() DOPathDG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysics DG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysicsDOPath
TweenerCore< Vector3, Path, PathOptions > DG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysics.DOPath (this Rigidbody target, Vector3[] path, float duration, PathType pathType = PathType::Linear , PathMode pathMode = PathMode::Full3D , int resolution = 10 , Color? gizmoColor = null )[static]
Tweens a Rigidbody's position through the given path waypoints, using the chosen path algorithm.
Also stores the Rigidbody as the tween's target so it can be used for filtered operations.
NOTE: to tween a rigidbody correctly it should be set to kinematic at least while being tweened.
BEWARE: doesn't work on Windows Phone store (waiting for Unity to fix their own bug). If you plan to publish there you should use a regular transform.DOPath.
Parameters
path
The waypoints to go through
duration
The duration of the tween
pathType
The type of path: Linear (straight path), CatmullRom (curved CatmullRom path) or CubicBezier (curved with control points)
pathMode
The path mode: 3D, side-scroller 2D, top-down 2D
resolution
The resolution of the path (useless in case of Linear paths): higher resolutions make for more detailed curved paths but are more expensive. Defaults to 10, but a value of 5 is usually enough if you don't have dramatic long curves between waypoints
gizmoColor
The color of the path (shown when gizmos are active in the Play panel and the tween is running)
DORotate() DORotateDG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysics DG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysicsDORotate
TweenerCore< Quaternion, Vector3, QuaternionOptions > DG.Tweening.DOTweenModulePhysics.DORotate (this Rigidbody target, Vector3 endValue, float duration, RotateMode mode = RotateMode::Fast )[static]
Tweens a Rigidbody's rotation to the given value.
Also stores the rigidbody as the tween's target so it can be used for filtered operations
Parameters
endValue
The end value to reach
duration
The duration of the tween
mode
Rotation mode
